issues.title: Stormwater Drainage Blocked
issues.problem: Stormwater drains in spine road and all internal roads are blocked due to excessive construction debris and rubbish from various construction activities by TVS. The shuttering laid during drain construction has not even been removed from inside.
issues.fix: Complete storm water drain system needs to be cleaned and debris needs to be cleared — not only the chambers. This clogging is due to TVS construction activity across the community.
issues.tvsResponse: As it is a part of routine Maintenance. The cost of maintaining will be deducted from maintenance charges.

issues.title: Unsecured OSR Fencing
issues.problem: The existing fencing allows the movement of stray dogs causing safety and hygiene issues to all residents and kids. There have been multiple dog chasing incidents in OSR 1. Dogs enter the Clubhouse premises through pocket fencing and get easy access to the swimming pool water.
issues.fix: PVC or equivalent fencing on top of existing fence to restrict dogs' movement and prevent reptiles from coming out of OSR — considering kids' and overall community safety.
issues.tvsResponse: Picket fencing has been completed in OSR‑1 and OSR‑3. As this is not a gated society, there is no commitment for a boundary wall. Picket fencing will be provided for OSR 2 as a demarcation.

issues.title: Man-made Pond — Civil Rework Required
issues.problem: 1. High snake-prone zone: This is a critical safety issue. During monsoon, snakes get driven from flowing water through the community drain system and hide/breed inside gaps in rocks, making them very difficult to catch. 2. During summer, the pond completely dries up as ground water level depletes, making it unusable.
issues.fix: 1. Civil rework to plaster the gaps or complete new brickwork with plastering to create a smooth surface — stopping snake hiding and breeding. 2. Open ground to be concreted so that water level depletion can be addressed. 3. Storm water drain to be connected directly onto the closed drain system to reduce snakes driven from flowing water.
issues.tvsResponse: Debris clearing activities will be carried out as part of maintenance.
issues.note: TVS only mentions debris clearing. Civil rework (plastering, brickwork) to prevent snake breeding — the core fix — has not been committed.

issues.title: Basement Soil Pile-up Near Phase 3
issues.problem: Basement soil pile-up near Phase 3 private land and inadequate compound wall height — a major security concern. Aaranya is built at natural soil level while adjacent roads/community are at higher latitude, causing flooding during monsoon. The elevated soil means any trespasser or stray animal can jump into the community. Despite having TVS Security and FM guards on duty, there was a recent trespasser incident due to the inadequate wall height.
issues.fix: The soil dump mess by TVS must be removed completely to natural soil level.
issues.tvsResponse: The accumulated soil will be disposed off appropriately.

issues.title: Perimeter Wall Too Short — Security Breach Risk
issues.problem: The height of the entire community perimeter wall is too short and prone to security breach. Trespassers can easily enter and jump into the community.
issues.fix: 6 feet compound wall clearance from both inside and outside areas, with barbed wire installed mandatory for the entire perimeter.
issues.reversalNote: CRITICAL: This core safety issue has been raised since the very first email in July 2025. After 9 months of escalation — including a physical visit by 40+ owners to TVS corporate office — the response is still only 'to be deliberated.' No commitment whatsoever.

issues.title: Security Gaps — Stray Dogs in OSR and Clubhouse
issues.problem: Stray dogs and animals are entering OSR areas and the clubhouse through gaps in picket fencing. This is a safety and hygiene issue for all residents and children.
issues.fix: PVC mesh installation to be provided in all picket fencing areas — OSR 1, OSR 2, OSR 3 and Clubhouse.
issues.tvsResponse: PVC mesh installation will be provided

issues.title: Paver Blocks Needed — Cherry, Kurinji, Ashoka Streets
issues.problem: Spots next to walkways in Cherry, Kurinji, and Ashoka streets need paver block installation. The bare ground is causing pest and reptile breeding issues.
issues.fix: Complete area needs to be paver block installed to avoid snakes and reptile breeding.
issues.tvsResponse: Paver blocks will be refilled and restored

issues.title: Cherry Street Garden Damaged
issues.problem: The Cherry Street garden stretching from Udanya Phase 2 to the main entrance arch has been completely damaged due to apartment compound wall work. The garden and electrical lines for street lights are damaged and electrical cables are exposed outside without proper safety measures.
issues.fix: Proper recabling of the street light electrical lines. All debris and rubbish in the pathway garden to be removed, red soil to be in place, and replanting required to restore the pathway garden.
issues.tvsResponse: Cherry Street Maintenance will be done as per schedule

issues.title: Swimming Pool Barricading — 8 Feet Height Required
issues.problem: Barricading was requested for 8 feet height, citing privacy issues for swimmers — especially women. The current height is inadequate and dogs are even able to jump through and access the swimming pool water for drinking.
issues.fix: 8 feet barricading as agreed during the last meeting.
issues.tvsResponse: TVS Emerald has already barricaded the swimming pool, further enhancements shall fall beyond our scope
issues.reversalNote: REVERSAL ALERT: The association requested 8 feet barricading as agreed during the last meeting. TVS is now claiming 'further enhancements are beyond our scope' — directly contradicting the commitment made in a prior meeting.

issues.title: Compound Wall Gap Near Pond — Outsiders Fishing
issues.problem: There is a compound wall gap near the pond area. Locals from outside the community are already entering through this gap and fishing in the pond — which is a safety concern for residents.
issues.fix: 6 feet compound wall to be constructed and the gap needs to be closed.
issues.tvsResponse: Cleaning of construction debris has already been initiated and will be completed as per the timeline
issues.note: TVS only mentions debris clearing. The 6-foot compound wall construction to seal the gap against trespassers has not been committed.

issues.title: OSR Gates — Spring/Rotary Retrofit Needed
issues.problem: 1. All OSR gates should be retrofitted with spring or rotary gates so they automatically close, remaining closed to prevent cattle and dog entry. 2. In OSR 1, the northern side of the park should have one rotary gate to enable park access for Phase 2 residents and entry after the clubhouse (near Kukum Street).
issues.fix: Current gates are not up to standard and definitely not a resort-style standard. Rotary gates need to be replaced at all OSR entries. One additional opening for OSR 1 near Kukum Street is required.
issues.tvsResponse: The proposed gate enhancements and installation of a rotary gate were not committed and hence are not included within the current scope of work
issues.reversalNote: REVERSAL ALERT: OSR gate security has been a recurring ask since July 2025 (first email). After 9 months on record, TVS formally declares it 'not committed' — despite the association raising security concerns repeatedly and TVS acknowledging stray dog issues in their own response (Item #12 commits PVC mesh). Contradictory position.
